prompt: We already have a GDPR compliance programme. What additional steps do we need to take to comply with CCPA/CPRA? Where do the two laws differ most significantly?
assertions: States that GDPR compliance provides a strong foundation (privacy notices, rights processes, processor agreements, data minimization) but does NOT automatically satisfy CCPA/CPRA
Identifies CCPA/CPRA-specific additions needed: 'Do Not Sell or Share My Personal Information' link + GPC signal compliance, and 'Limit the Use of My Sensitive Personal Information' link (CPRA)
Explains the opt-out vs. opt-in model difference: CCPA/CPRA is primarily opt-out (consumers must request opt-out) whereas GDPR requires opt-in consent for most sensitive processing
Notes that CCPA/CPRA has no lawful basis requirement for general PI processing — businesses can collect PI without one of GDPR's six legal bases

Highlights the CCPA/CPRA private right of action for data breaches ($100–$750 per consumer per incident) which has no GDPR equivalent, and the need to verify vendor contracts meet CCPA service provider requirements
